data.name: Cycle 3 and Subsequent Odd Cycle Visit Window
ECG Predose Window Cycle 1 Day 1 and Cycle 2 Day 1
ECG Postdose Window Cycle 1 Day 1 and Cycle 2 Day 1
Steroid Increase Exclusion Window for Brain Metastases
Other Brain Metastases Treatment Exclusion Window
Participation in Another Clinical Trial Exclusion Window
Contraception Duration After Last Dose for Females
Contraception Duration After Last Dose for Males
Primary Analysis Cutoff Cohort A and B (Minimum Treatment Duration)
Primary Analysis Cutoff Cohort C (Minimum Treatment Duration)
Visit Schedule First 9 Months (Every Cycle Day 1)
Visit Schedule After 9 Months (Every Second Cycle Day 1)
METex14 Skipping Alteration Positive
data.description: Subject tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations in plasma and/or tissue
data.text: METex14 skipping alterations in plasma and/or tissue, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status

data.name: MET Amplification Positive (LBx)
data.description: Subject tested positive for MET amplification by liquid biopsy
data.text: MET amplification only in plasma defined by a positive LBx test, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status

data.name: MET Amplification Positive (TBx)
data.description: Subject tested positive for MET amplification in tissue with GCN gain of at least 4
data.text: Based on the outcome of the interim analysis in 12 LBx selected subjects: MET amplification only in tissue defined by a positive TBx with a gain of at least 4 copies of the MET gene

data.name: Futility Analysis Criteria for Cohort B
data.description: At least 3 out of 12 subjects need to respond (CR + PR) to avoid stopping for futility
data.text: In order not to stop for futility, at least 3 out of 12 subjects need to respond (CR + PR). Recruitment will continue while the futility analysis is performed.

data.name: ECOG Performance Status Eligibility
data.description: Subject must have ECOG PS of 0 or 1
data.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status (ECOG PS) of 0 or 1

data.name: Measurable Disease by IRC
data.description: Subject must have measurable disease confirmed by independent review
data.text: Measurable disease by IRC in accordance with RECIST Version 1.1

data.name: Disease Progression per RECIST 1.1
data.description: Disease progression as assessed according to RECIST Version 1.1
data.text: Disease progression according to RECIST Version 1.1

data.name: Adequate Hematological Function
data.description: Subject must meet minimum hematological thresholds
data.text: Hemoglobin >= 8.5 g/dL, Neutrophils >= 1.5 × 10^9/L, Platelets >= 100 × 10^9/L

data.name: Prior Therapy Lines Limit
data.description: Treatment naïve or pretreated with no more than 2 prior lines of therapy
data.text: Treatment naïve patients in first-line or pretreated patients with no more than 2 lines of prior therapy

data.name: No EGFR Activating Mutations
data.description: Subject must not have characterized EGFR activating mutations predicting sensitivity to anti-EGFR therapy
data.text: Subjects with characterized EGFR activating mutations that predict sensitivity to anti-EGFR therapy are excluded

data.name: No ALK Rearrangements
data.description: Subject must not have characterized ALK rearrangements predicting sensitivity to anti-ALK therapy
data.text: Subjects with characterized ALK rearrangements that predict sensitivity to anti-ALK therapy are excluded

data.name: AE Recovery for Dose Modification
data.description: AE related to tepotinib must recover to Grade 2 or less or baseline values
data.text: Continue tepotinib treatment at a lower dose level until the AE related to tepotinib recovers to ≤Grade 2 or to baseline values

data.name: Unresolved Toxicity Exclusion
data.description: No unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more from previous anticancer therapy
data.text: Any unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more according to NCI-CTCAE Version 4.03, from previous anticancer therapy

data.name: End of Trial Definition
data.description: All subjects discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of subjects in each cohort have died
data.text: The end of the trial is defined as the time point at which all subjects have discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of the subjects in each of the 3 cohorts, respectively, have died.

data.name: Negative Pregnancy Test
data.description: Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test prior to enrollment
data.text: Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test (β-HCG test in serum) prior to enrollment

data.name: Prescreening to Screening Transition
data.text: Subject must have confirmed MET alteration status (METex14 skipping alterations or MET amplification) by central laboratory or assay with appropriate regulatory status. Subjects must sign a separate main ICF.

data.name: Screening to Treatment Transition
data.text: Subject must meet all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria. Screening procedures must be performed within 28 days before first dose. Time window from enrollment to first dose is 72 hours.

data.name: Treatment to End of Treatment Transition
data.text: End of Treatment visit will occur within 14 days of last dose of trial treatment upon disease progression (RECIST 1.1), death, AE leading to discontinuation, or withdrawal of consent.

data.name: End of Treatment to Safety Follow-up Transition
data.text: 30-day follow-up visit should be performed at 30 ± 3 days after last dose of trial treatment for all subjects who discontinue trial treatment permanently, including subjects who have completed an End of Treatment visit.

data.name: Discontinuation for Disease Progression
data.text: Documented progression of the disease according to RECIST Version 1.1 requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Adverse Event
data.text: Occurrence of AEs, if discontinuation of trial treatment is desired or considered necessary by the Investigator and/or the subject.

data.name: Discontinuation for Consent Withdrawal
data.text: Subject withdraws consent. Subjects may withdraw from the trial at any time without giving a reason.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Occurrence of pregnancy requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Non-Compliance (Treatment Interruption Exceeded)
data.text: Non-compliance with administration of tepotinib. The maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption is 21 days.

data.name: Discontinuation for Prohibited Concomitant Drug
data.text: Use of a non-permitted concomitant drug where the predefined consequence is withdrawal from tepotinib.

data.name: Discontinuation for Initiation of Other Anticancer Treatment
data.text: Initiation of any other anticancer treatment (including radiotherapy, surgery, hormonal therapy) requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Discontinuation for Therapeutic Failure
data.text: Therapeutic failure (i.e., oncologic emergency due to serious tumor progression or serious side effect) requiring urgent additional therapy.

data.name: Dose Reduction Modification
data.text: Dose reduced from 500 mg to 250 mg once daily (standard dose reduction after protocol Version 8.0). If subject does not tolerate 250 mg once daily or AE does not resolve following treatment interruption, permanent treatment discontinuation should be discussed with the Sponsor.

data.name: Treatment Interruption Modification
data.text: Maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption is 21 days. Following a treatment interruption, subjects can be re-challenged at the initial dose level of 500 mg.

data.name: Dose Re-escalation
data.text: Following dose modifications, the dose of tepotinib may be increased again to 500 mg at the discretion of the Investigator.

data.name: Treatment Discontinuation to Additional Follow-up (Non-PD)
data.text: Subjects who withdraw from treatment for reasons other than PD have additional visits for tumor assessments. Assessments continue until disease progression, withdrawal of consent, or death.

data.name: Cohort B Futility Interim Analysis Decision
data.text: Based on outcome of the interim analysis in 12 selected subjects tested positive for MET amplification by LBx, if at least 3 out of 12 subjects respond (CR + PR), enrollment continues to enrich LBx cohort to 60 subjects and 2 additional TBx analysis sets may be started.

data.name: Premature Trial Termination by Sponsor or Health Authorities
data.text: The clinical trial may be terminated prematurely or suspended at the request of Health Authorities or if new safety or efficacy information leads to an unfavorable risk benefit judgment for tepotinib. The Sponsor may discontinue the trial due to futility, if it becomes unjustifiable for medical or ethical reasons, for poor enrollment, or because of discontinuation of clinical development.

data.name: Exclusion Criterion Occurrence During Treatment
data.text: Occurrence of an exclusion criterion which is clinically relevant and affects the subject's safety, if discontinuation is considered necessary by the Investigator and/or Sponsor.

data.name: Participation in Another Clinical Trial
data.text: Participation in another clinical trial will be considered withdrawal from the trial.

data.name: Disease Progression
data.description: Subject discontinues treatment upon documented disease progression according to RECIST Version 1.1.

data.name: Adverse Event Leading to Discontinuation
data.description: Subject discontinues treatment due to adverse event considered unacceptable by the Investigator and/or subject.

data.name: Withdrawal of Consent
data.description: Subject may withdraw from the trial at any time without giving a reason. Subjects who are withdrawn for any reason must not re-enter this trial.

data.name: Death
data.description: Subject death during the trial.

data.name: Treatment Interruption Exceeds 21 Days
data.description: Non-compliance with administration of tepotinib where the maximum permitted period of continuous treatment interruption of 21 days is exceeded.

data.name: Intolerance to Lowest Dose Level
data.description: If a subject still does not tolerate the 250 mg once daily dose, or the AE does not resolve following treatment interruption, permanent treatment discontinuation should be discussed with the Sponsor.

data.name: Pregnancy
data.description: Occurrence of pregnancy requires immediate withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Premature Trial Termination by Sponsor
data.description: The Sponsor may discontinue the trial due to futility, unfavorable risk-benefit, poor enrollment, discontinuation of clinical development, or withdrawal from market for safety reasons.

data.name: Study Completion - End of Trial
data.description: End of trial defined as when all subjects have discontinued trial drug and two-thirds of the subjects in each of the 3 cohorts have died. Final analysis including efficacy and safety will be conducted.

data.name: Lost to Follow-up
data.description: Subject lost to follow-up requires withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Safety Concern Exit
data.description: Any events that unacceptably endanger the safety of the subject require withdrawal from tepotinib therapy.

data.name: Cohort B Futility Interim Analysis
data.description: Futility analysis in initial 12 LBx-tested subjects in Cohort B. At least 3 out of 12 subjects must respond (CR + PR) to continue enrollment. Determines enrichment of LBx cohort to 60 subjects and start of exploratory TBx cohorts.

data.name: Tumor Assessment Response Decision (First 9 Months)
data.description: Tumor assessments every 6 weeks following Cycle 1 Day 1 until 9 months per RECIST 1.1. Decision to continue or discontinue treatment based on disease progression.

data.name: Tumor Assessment Response Decision (After 9 Months)
data.description: Tumor assessments every 12 weeks after 9 months per RECIST 1.1. Decision to continue or discontinue treatment based on disease progression.

data.name: Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Decision to temporarily interrupt treatment, reduce dose to 250 mg, or permanently discontinue based on AE severity and recovery to Grade 2 or less or baseline values.

data.name: Primary Analysis Decision Cohort A and B
data.description: Primary analysis conducted once all subjects in each primary analysis set have been treated for at least 6 months, died, or prematurely discontinued trial treatment.

data.name: Primary Analysis Decision Cohort C
data.description: Primary analysis conducted once all subjects in each primary analysis set have been treated for at least 9 months, died, or permanently discontinued trial treatment.

data.name: Rescreening Decision
data.description: Rescreening is permitted only for subjects with confirmed METex14 alterations or MET amplification if they do not meet one of the other inclusion or exclusion criteria and after discussion with the Sponsor.
